title: slokit SLO spec (sloth prometheus/v1 compatible)
description: A sloth (https://sloth.dev) prometheus/v1-compatible SLO spec with the slokit extensions: per-SLO `period`, the `latency` SLI, `sli.plugin`, and `alerting.windows`. This schema encodes structural constraints only: required fields, enum values, the exclusive SLI choice, duration and metric-name patterns, and objective bounds. Cross-field semantics stay in `slokit validate`, which remains authoritative: duplicate SLO names within and across specs, `short` shorter than `long` in alerting.windows, rejection of zero-total durations such as `0s`, comma and quote checks on latency selectors, plugin id resolution and option contracts, and the `{{.window}}` token check on plugin-expanded queries. Unknown properties are allowed everywhere, matching the parser: newer sloth keys do not break slokit.

version.description: Spec version. slokit ignores this field and always generates prometheus/v1 rules; `slokit lint` warns (SPEC_VERSION) when it is not `prometheus/v1`.

service.description: The service these SLOs describe. Must not be empty or whitespace-only.

labels.description: Labels propagated onto every generated rule for this service.

slos.description: The SLOs for this service. SLO names must be unique within the service; `slokit validate` enforces uniqueness, including across the spec files of one run.

labelMap.description: Names to string values (labels or annotations). Names must not be empty; `slokit lint` additionally warns on names outside the legacy Prometheus charset (LABEL_NAME_CHARS) and on user labels with the reserved `sloth_` prefix (RESERVED_LABEL).

duration.description: A Prometheus-style duration: one or more number-unit terms, summed (`30m`, `1h30m`, `2w`). Units: s, m, h, d, w. `slokit validate` rejects zero-total durations such as `0s`.

windowedQuery.description: A PromQL query template containing the window token, written `{{.window}}` or `{{ .window }}` (the two forms generation substitutes), which is replaced with each burn-rate window.

name.description: SLO name, unique within the service. Must not be empty or whitespace-only.

objective.description: Reliability target as a percentage in the open interval (0, 100), e.g. `99.9`.

description.description: Human-readable description of the SLO (`slokit lint` reports NO_DESCRIPTION when absent).

labels.description: Labels added to this SLO's rules.

period.description: slokit extension: per-SLO period override (e.g. `28d`). Defaults to 30d (or the CLI `--period`) when absent; the burn-rate windows scale to it unless `--no-period-scaling` is set.

sli.description: How the error ratio is measured: exactly one of `events`, `raw`, `latency`, or `plugin`.

eventsSli.description: Events-based SLI: bad events over total events.

rawSli.description: Raw SLI: a query that already yields an error ratio.

error_ratio_query.description: Query yielding an error ratio over `{{.window}}`.

latencySli.description: slokit extension: the fraction of requests slower than a histogram bucket threshold. slokit generates the bucket math (`1 - bucket(le=threshold) / count`), so you do not hand-write it.

histogram_metric.description: Histogram base metric without the `_bucket`/`_count` suffix. Embedded unquoted in the generated PromQL, so it must match the classic Prometheus metric-name charset.

threshold.description: The `le` bucket boundary as a string (e.g. "0.3"), embedded verbatim in the `le="..."` matcher: a positive number with no surrounding whitespace. `slokit validate` also rejects zero.

selector.description: Optional label matchers written without braces, e.g. `job="api"`. `slokit validate` also checks quote balance and leading/trailing commas.

pluginSli.description: sloth-compatible plugin SLI shape, resolved against slokit's own SLI plugin registry. slokit never loads sloth's Go plugin files, so `sloth-common/...` ids fail validation with an unknown-plugin-id error.

id.description: Registry key, e.g. `slokit/availability/http-requests-total`. Whether the id exists (and its option contract) is checked by `slokit validate`.

options.description: Option values passed to the plugin. Scalars only: strings, numbers, and booleans are coerced to strings (`threshold: 0.5` equals `threshold: "0.5"`). Option names a plugin does not declare are an advisory lint (PLUGIN_UNKNOWN_OPTION).

alerting.description: Alerting metadata for the page and ticket burn-rate alerts.

name.description: Alert rule name; falls back to the SLO name when empty. Must not be whitespace-only.

windows.description: slokit extension: custom burn-rate conditions replacing the default MWMBR window table for this SLO. An empty or absent list keeps the (period-scaled) defaults. `slokit lint` warns when custom windows leave an enabled severity with no conditions (NO_SEVERITY_WINDOWS) or repeat a condition (DUPLICATE_ALERT_WINDOW).

alertWindow.description: One custom burn-rate condition: the alert for `severity` fires when the error ratio exceeds `factor` times the budget over both the `long` and `short` windows.

severity.description: Which alert this condition belongs to.

long.description: The long lookback window, e.g. `1h`. Must be longer than `short` (`slokit validate` checks the comparison).

factor.description: The burn-rate multiplier that triggers this condition.
